item.title: Consumers still suffer in getting fuel oil
item.description: Consumers on Wednesday continued to suffer in getting fuel oil across Bangladesh with a little improvement in the situation in the Dhaka city as the government refrained from hiking prices of petroleum products......Politics

item.title: Oil-laden train derails in Habiganj, rail links with Sylhet suspended
item.description: Six wagons of an oil-laden train derailed in Habiganj on Wednesday night, disrupting rail communication between Sylhet and the rest of the country....

item.title: Italy in hell!
item.description: Italians will once again be forced to watch a World Cup from the sidelines after another play-off disaster highlighted just how far one of the great footballing nations has fallen......

item.title: What the press said...
item.description: ‘The nightmare continues: Italy is experiencing its third apocalypse, one that is even worse than the previous two. Italy has lost its sense of shock, of catastrophe. Missing out on the World Cup is becoming the norm. We won’t be able to talk about a World Cup again until around 2030, 16 years after our last World Cup appearance. A whole......

item.title: Bangladesh women suffer heartbreak on debut
item.description: Debutant Bangladesh squandered a two-goal lead to suffer a 3-2 defeat to hosts Thailand in their opening Group A match of the AFC Under-20 Women’s Asian Cup at the Thammasat Stadium in Thammasat, Bangkok, on Wednesday......Diaspora community news

item.title: Political economy of institutional accountability
item.description: In recent decades, Bangladesh has made significant progress across economic, social, and human development indicators. Yet one in five people still live below the poverty line, with higher rates in rural areas and persistent inequality. These disparities shape not only access to services, but also who is able to participate, speak and be heard within accountability......

item.title: Strengthening consumer protections
item.description: IN BANGLADESH today, e-commerce is transforming the way people shop. With a growing number of consumers using online marketplaces, social media pages, and other digital platforms to purchase goods, shopping has never been easier. Yet this convenience comes with new risks. Customers may receive defective items, fail to receive products they paid for......

item.title: Environment is, nature isn’t
item.description: WHEN the first light of morning gently breaks our sleep and we open the window, the world seems to stand before us. But do we truly recognise it? The fresh scent of soil is absent. There is, instead, the sharpness of dust. The soft flutter of bird wings is gone. In its place echoes the harsh roar of engines. From a distance comes the shrill cry of car horns, the relentless noise......

item.title: Strengthening health system from within
item.description: BANGLADESH’S public health achievements are often commended internationally — and rightly so. Over the past three decades, the country has significantly reduced maternal and child mortality, expanded immunisation coverage and increased life expectancy. These accomplishments demonstrate that determined policy efforts can deliver major health gains even with limited resources......

item.title: Internal trade barriers
item.description: BANGLADESH has made significant economic progress over the past decades, driven by export growth, industrialisation and poverty reduction. However, behind this success lies a complex network of internal trade barriers that limit market efficiency, raise transaction costs and weaken overall competitiveness. Internal trade, which involves the movement of goods......

item.title: Govt needs to act to mend level crossings
item.description: THIS is deplorable that the authorities continue to ignore the threat that unattended and unauthorised level crossings pose. Despite regular accidents and deaths on level crossings, nearly 80 per cent of such crossings are still unattended and almost half are unauthorised. It appears that the railway network has normalised danger at every turn. These crossings, many......

item.title: Students must not pay for power austerity
item.description: THE government’s plan to hold classes in educational institutions partially online as part of a set of steps to save power and strengthen energy austerity measures, already in place, raises certain concern. This so does as the online classroom teaching introduced during the Covid outbreak in 2020 seriously disrupted the education of 37 million children, causing learning losses......

item.title: 9 killed in road accidents in 3 districts
item.description: At least nine people were killed and four others injured in road accidents in Cumilla, Jhenaidah and Naogaon on Wednesday......

item.title: 2 students injured as ceiling collapses at JnU, protests erupt over safety concerns
item.description: Two students were injured after a portion of the ceiling plaster collapsed inside a seminar room at Jagannath University on Wednesday morning, triggering protests on campus over long-standing infrastructure issues......

item.title: Rajshahi people demand Padma Barrage to prevent desertification
item.description: People in Rajshahi city formed a human chain on Wednesday, demanding the speedy implementation of the long-pending Padma Barrage project to address growing water scarcity and save the northern region from desertification......

item.title: Sazu Khadem’s exhibition ‘Reflections Within’ under way in US
item.description: The Pflugerville Arts Council has organised a 28-day exhibition of actor and visual artist Sazu Khadem titled Reflections Within under way at the Pflugerville Public Library at Austin, Texas in the United States......

item.title: Aranyak Theatre Pathshala stages Ganer Pakhi
item.description: The 23rd batch of Aranyak Theatre Pathshala, a schooling programme of Aranyak Natyadal, staged its final production titled Ganer Pakhi at the Studio Theatre Hall of Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy in Dhaka on Wednesday......Diaspora community news

item.title: Two hospitals fined in Noakhali for keeping expired medicines
item.description: A mobile court fined two private hospitals for keeping expired medicines at Chatkhil in Noakhali on Wednesday......

item.title: Southeast University celebrates Independence Day
item.description: The Southeast University celebrated Independence Day with due respect and enthusiasm by organising a discussion session and cultural programme at the Multipurpose Hall of the university in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Training on HEAT project’s financial management begins at GAU
item.description: A two-day training programme titled Financial Management for Sub-Projects under the Higher Education Acceleration and Transformation project began at Gazipur Agricultural University on Wednesday......

item.title: Dhaka committed to boosting strategic ties with Tokyo: Zahid
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister Abu Zafar Md Zahid Hossain has said the friendship between Bangladesh and Japan is deeply rooted in mutual trust, common aspirations and progress......Geographic Reference

item.title: Four districts get new DCs
item.description: The ministry of public administration on Wednesday appointed new deputy commissioners in four districts......

item.title: Govt shifting to investment-led, inclusive growth: finance minister
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Wednesday outlined a comprehensive strategy to navigate what he described as a ‘triple crisis’ facing the national economy, emphasising a shift from debt-dependency to an investment-led growth model while prioritizing social inclusion in the forthcoming budget......

item.title: Parliamentary body soon for constitutional amendments: chief whip
item.description: Chief whip Nurul Islam Moni on Wednesday said a special parliamentary committee will soon be formed to facilitate constitutional amendments......

item.title: Govt declares April 9 holiday for by-polls
item.description: The government has declared a general holiday on April 9 in the areas concerned of Bogura and Sherpur districts on the occasion of by-elections to two vacant parliamentary seats......

item.title: Bangla Academy observed Independence Day
item.description: Bangla Academy observed the 56th Independence and National Day at its Kabi Shamsur Rahman seminar room on Tuesday, according to a press release......

item.title: Court dismisses Sanjay’s bail plea in Hadi murder case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday dismissed the bail petition of Sanjay Chisim, accused of assisting prime suspect Foysal Karim Masud and his accomplice Alamgir Hossain in fleeing to India following the murder of Inquilab Mancha spokesperson Sharif Osman Hadi......

item.title: WB divisional director visits PRAN-RFL training centre
item.description: World Bank divisional director Jean Pesme visited the PRAN-RFL Factory and Training Centre in Gazipur on Wednesday to inspect the enterprise-based training programme conducted in collaboration with the Asset Project of the Directorate of Technical Education......

item.title: Bangladesh, KSA discuss boosting manpower co-operation
item.description: Bangladesh and Kingdom of Saudi Arabia discussed expanding manpower export and recognising Bangladeshi skills to strengthen bilateral labour cooperation during a meeting held in Geneva......Diaspora community news

item.title: BARI holds workshop on SACP project
item.description: A workshop on progress review of BARI activities under Smallholder Agricultural Competitiveness Project held at the seminar room of Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute in Gazipur on Tuesday......

item.title: ASSET project inaugurates training programme
item.description: A skill development training programme for chief instructors, instructors, workshop superintendents, junior instructors and craft instructors from various polytechnic institutes and technical schools and colleges of the country was inaugurated at the Bangladesh Industrial Technical Assistance Centre on Tuesday......

item.title: Food supply at 20 govt daycare centres disrupted
item.description: Parents have been asked to bring food for their children on their own as food supply for children at 20 government-run daycare centers was suspended following unpaid bills on Wednesday......

item.title: Two killed in lightning strikes in 2 districts
item.description: At least two persons were killed and three others injured in separate lightning strikes in Habiganj and Sylhet districts on Wednesday......

item.title: Schoolgirl drowns in Naogaon
item.description: A 10-year-old girl drowned in the Atrai River in Manda upazila of Naogaon on Wednesday while taking a bath......

item.title: Twins killed in Faridpur train accident
item.description: Twin children were killed when a train struck them at Lakhandia rail gate in Madhukhali upazila of Faridpur Wednesday afternoon......

item.title: Deliver agricultural services timely to farmers: Azizul Islam
item.description: Chairman of the Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation Md Azizul Islam has made an urge to deliver the services provided by BADC timely to farmers......Education resources Bangladesh

item.title: BMU calls for online appointments to avoid crowding at OPDs
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University on Wednesday made a call to bring the print tickets for online appointments to reduce the crowding and suffering of patients for its outpatients departments 1 and 2......

item.title: Ignoring referendum verdict may trigger political crisis: Rob
item.description: Jatiya Samajtantrik Dal–JSD president ASM Abdur Rob on Wednesday said that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government’s move to ignore the referendum verdict on the July National Charter could create dangerouspoliticalconsequences in the country......

item.title: SC hearing landmark citizenship case with Trump in audience
item.description: The US Supreme Court was weighing Donald Trump’s historic bid to end birthright citizenship on Wednesday — with the Republican president smashing protocol by sitting in the audience......

item.title: 29 killed as Russian military plane crashes in Crimea
item.description: A Russian Antonov-26 military plane crashed while flying over the occupied Crimean peninsula, killing 29 people, the TASS state news agency reported Wednesday, quoting Russia’s defence ministry......

item.title: Pakistan, Afghanistan hold talks to end conflict
item.description: Pakistan and Afghanistan are holding talks in China to try to find a way to end months of conflict sparked by cross-border attacks, two officials from Islamabad said on Wednesday......Politics

item.title: ICT prosecution investigates bribery allegations
item.description: International Crimes Tribunal’s chief prosecutor Md Aminul Islam on Wednesday said that a fact-finding committee had recently launched an investigation into serious corruption allegations, including the entry of an accused person’s wife into prosecutor Gazi Monowar Hossain Tamim’s office carrying a briefcase......

item.title: Fellows demand justice for killed JU student
item.description: Jahangirnagar University teachers and students staged a demonstration on the campus on Wednesday, demanding swift justice over the killing of a final-year student, allegedly by her husband at a rented house near the campus......

item.title: Photographer Shahidul Alam calls on DU VC
item.description: Internationally renowned photographer and founder of Pathshala South Asian Media Institute Shahidul Alam paid a courtesy call on Dhaka University vice-chancellor Professor ABM Obaidul Islam at the VC office on Wednesday......

item.title: UGC’s HEAT project implementation progress moderately ‘satisfactory’: WB
item.description: The World Bank has assessed the overall implementation progress of the Higher Education Acceleration and Transformation project being implemented by the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh as moderately satisfactory......Geographic Reference

item.title: 175 Bangladeshis return from Libya
item.description: One hundred and seventy five Bangladeshi nationals returned home from Libya on Wednesday......

item.title: ILO assures of cooperation in creating employment in Bangladesh
item.description: The International Labour Organisation has assured of cooperation in creating employment in Bangladesh, aligning with government priorities......

item.title: Iran disappointed by Bangladesh statement not condemning US aggression
item.description: Ambassador of the Islamic Republic of Iran to Bangladesh Jalil Rahimi Jahanabadi on Wednesday said that Iran was disappointed by the statements issued by Bangladesh over the ongoing conflict in Gulf states following the United States and Israel’s joint attack on Iran......

item.title: Probe report deadline extended for 125th time
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday deferred the submission of the investigation report into the murder of journalist couple Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi for the 125th time, setting May 7 as the new date......Education resources Bangladesh

item.title: Bangladesh expresses solidarity with Oman
item.description: Bangladesh on Wednesday expressed solidarity with the people of Oman amid the ongoing conflict in the Gulf states following the United States-Israel attack on Iran and retaliatory strikes from Tehran on US bases across the Arab countries......

item.title: ICT prosecution, DB discuss probe into torture cases
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police’s Detective Branch chief Md Shafiqul Islam on Wednesday met International Crimes Tribunal chief prosecutor Md Aminul Islam at the latter’s office to discuss cases related to alleged torture and political persecution during the military-backed caretaker government, widely known as the ‘1/11’ government that held power between......

item.title: Wave of US-Israel, Iran strikes continues
item.description: Tehran was rocked by a fresh wave of explosions on Wednesday, as US president Donald Trump said the war with Iran could be over in two or three weeks......

item.title: 23,865 political cases withdrawn
item.description: Law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Wednesday said 23,865 politically motivated cases to harass people have so far been withdrawn across the country......

item.title: One more dies of measles
item.description: At least one child died of measles and 513 others were admitted to hospitals with symptoms across Bangladesh in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Wednesday, according to the Directorate General of Health Services......
